evals.name: create-minimal-plugin
evals.prompt: Create a minimal Claude Code plugin called 'code-health' with a single /check-health command that runs linting and test coverage checks.
evals.expected_output: A plugin directory with correct structure: .claude-plugin/plugin.json + commands/check-health.md

assertions.text: Command has YAML frontmatter with description

evals.name: plugin-with-skill-and-command
evals.prompt: Build a plugin called 'api-docs' with: (1) a skill that auto-triggers when the user asks about API documentation, and (2) a /generate-docs command that explicitly generates OpenAPI docs from code.
evals.expected_output: Plugin with both skills/api-docs/SKILL.md and commands/generate-docs.md, plus plugin.json

assertions.text: Command references the skill via CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT

evals.name: add-hook-to-plugin
evals.prompt: Add a SessionStart hook to my existing plugin at ./my-plugin/ that runs a setup script when the session begins. The script should check if dependencies are installed.
evals.expected_output: hooks/hooks.json created with SessionStart event and a command hook pointing to a setup script

evals.name: add-agent-with-restrictions
evals.prompt: Add a read-only code analysis agent to my plugin. It should use haiku for speed, only have Read/Grep/Glob tools, and analyze code quality when asked.
evals.expected_output: agents/code-analyzer.md with model: haiku, restricted tools, and clear instructions

assertions.text: Does NOT include Write or Bash in tools

evals.name: mcp-limitation-awareness
evals.prompt: I want to create a plugin that scans Gmail for meeting invites and adds them to Google Calendar. Design the plugin architecture.
evals.expected_output: Agent recognizes MCP tools are needed and keeps the workflow in a skill/command (not an agent), documenting the MCP limitation

assertions.text: Keeps MCP work in skill/command not agent
Does not create an agent for MCP work
